message:
feat: bytes literal support and __bool__ validation for Python 3 compliance

- Add `.bytes` variant to AST Value type for bytes literals (b'...')
- Bytes literals now correctly emit raw bytes without UTF-8 encoding of \xNN
- __bool__ methods return PythonError!bool to validate return type
- __bool__ takes precedence over __base_value__ for subclassed types
- __len__ returning negative now raises ValueError
- boolBuiltinCall properly propagates errors from __bool__/__len__
- 26 tests now pass in test_bool.py

files.patch:
@@ -29,7 +29,9 @@ pub const ComptimeEvaluator = struct {
                 .float => |f| ComptimeValue{ .float = f },
                 .bool => |b| ComptimeValue{ .bool = b },
                 .string => |s| ComptimeValue{ .string = s },
+                .bytes => |s| ComptimeValue{ .string = s }, // Bytes treated as string for comptime
                 .none => null, // None cannot be compile-time evaluated
+                .complex => null, // Complex cannot be compile-time evaluated as simple value
             },
             .binop => |op| self.evalBinOp(op),
             .unaryop => |op| self.evalUnaryOp(op),
